title: tibetiodes brevis
side_effects: Tibetiodes brevis can have side effects if used improperly, such as nausea, dizziness, and headaches due to its high concentration of toxic compounds. Ingestion of large quantities can lead to more severe symptoms, including vomiting, diarrhea, and abdominal pain. Prolonged exposure to the plant's volatile oils can cause respiratory issues, such as congestion and shortness of breath. Improper handling and preparation of the plant can also result in skin irritation and allergic reactions.
